steps.title: Find the base width from the base area
steps.content:
Base area $$= l \times w$$. Given base area $$= 16t$$ cm² and length $$l = \frac{8}{3}$$ cm:
$$16t = \frac{8}{3} \cdot w \implies w = 16t \cdot \frac{3}{8} = 6t \text{ cm}$$

steps.title: Set up the surface area formula
steps.content:
A right rectangular prism has six faces forming three pairs. With $$l = \frac{8}{3}$$, $$w = 6t$$, $$h = 9$$:
$$SA = 2lw + 2lh + 2wh$$

steps.title: Compute each pair of faces
steps.content:
• Two bases: $$2lw = 2 \times 16t = 32t$$ cm²
  • Two length-height faces: $$2lh = 2 \times \frac{8}{3} \times 9 = 2 \times 24 = 48$$ cm²
  • Two width-height faces: $$2wh = 2 \times 6t \times 9 = 108t$$ cm²
$$SA = 32t + 48 + 108t = 140t + 48 \text{ cm}^2$$

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices
steps.content:
• "$32t + 96$": Uses $2lh = 96$, which would require $lh = 48$; but $\frac{8}{3} \times 9 = 24$, so $2lh = 48$, not 96. Also missing the $108t$ term. Eliminated.
  • "$140t + 48$": Matches $32t + 108t + 48 = 140t + 48$. Correct.
  • "$800t + 48$": The variable coefficient is far too large; no combination of the given dimensions produces it. Eliminated.
  • "$144t$": Purely variable with no constant term, but the length-height faces contribute a constant 48. Eliminated.
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
